rationale: The cited paper appears theoretically central rather than incidental: it develops the choice-construction vs preference-construction account using attraction/compromise contexts, which directly overlaps with the citing paper’s focal constructs (preference construction, dominance/decoy context effects, and strategy use). The citing note explicitly frames its contribution as extending context-effects work via dominance-based strategy stickiness, making this pair a plausible substantive theory/mechanism link worth full second-stage judgment.
